annotations.label: Euclid's parallel postulate

anchor.snippet: Euclid's parallel line postulate

annotations.note: Mathlib builds Euclidean geometry on real inner-product/affine spaces (with AffineSubspace.Parallel) rather than stating Euclid's parallel postulate as an axiom.

annotations.label: Thales' theorem

anchor.snippet: Thales' Theorem

annotations.note: Thales' theorem (angle inscribed in a semicircle is a right angle) is stated as `angle_eq_pi_div_two_iff_mem_sphere_of_isDiameter` with alias `thales_theorem`.

annotations.label: Pythagorean theorem

anchor.snippet: the Pythagorean theorem

annotations.note: The Pythagorean theorem appears in angle-at-point form and vector forms in the right-angle file.

annotations.label: Platonic solids

anchor.snippet: the Platonic solids

annotations.note: No definition or classification of regular polyhedra/Platonic solids exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Squaring the circle (Antiphon)

anchor.snippet: construct a rectilinear figure with the same area as a given circle

annotations.note: Mathlib has no theory of straightedge-and-compass construction nor a squaring-the-circle statement.

annotations.label: Squaring the circle by neusis (Hippias)

anchor.snippet: a method for squaring a circle with a neusis construction

annotations.note: No neusis construction or quadratrix is formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Line meeting a circle at a single point

anchor.snippet: the possibility for a line to intersect a circle at a single point

annotations.note: `IsTangentAt.mem_and_mem_iff_eq` proves a tangent affine subspace meets the sphere only at the tangent point, in any dimension.

annotations.label: Cone is one-third of cylinder (Democritus)

anchor.snippet: the area of a cone was 1/3 the area of a cylinder with the same base

annotations.note: Mathlib has no geometric cone/cylinder volume formulas (`cone` in Mathlib refers to category-theory limit cones).

annotations.label: Doubling the cube

anchor.section: Mathematics in the time of Plato
anchor.snippet: the problem of doubling the cube

annotations.note: No constructibility theory or doubling-the-cube impossibility result exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Irrationality of square roots from 3 to 17

anchor.section: Mathematics in the time of Plato
anchor.snippet: the square roots of several numbers from 3 to 17 are irrational

annotations.note: `irrational_sqrt_natCast_iff` shows √n is irrational iff n is not a perfect square, covering all non-squares from 3 to 17 (also via a norm_num extension).

annotations.label: Spiral of Theodorus

anchor.section: Mathematics in the time of Plato
anchor.snippet: the construction now known as the Spiral of Theodorus

annotations.note: No definition of the Spiral of Theodorus exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Incommensurable magnitudes

anchor.section: Mathematics in the time of Plato
anchor.snippet: incommensurable magnitudes

annotations.note: Mathlib's `Irrational` captures incommensurability of a magnitude with the unit, but there is no general notion of two magnitudes being incommensurable (Mathlib's `Commensurable` concerns subgroups).

annotations.label: Exactly five regular polyhedra exist

anchor.section: Mathematics in the time of Plato
anchor.snippet: the proof that only five of them exist

annotations.note: Mathlib has no classification of regular polyhedra, so the 'only five Platonic solids' theorem is absent.

annotations.label: Eudoxus' theory of proportion

anchor.section: Mathematics in the time of Plato
anchor.snippet: the theory of proportion found in Book V of the Elements

annotations.note: Mathlib uses the modern ordered-field real numbers rather than formalizing Eudoxus' Book V theory of proportion.

annotations.label: Volume of a cone (Eudoxus)

anchor.section: Mathematics in the time of Plato
anchor.snippet: the volume of a cone is one-third the volume of a cylinder with the same base

annotations.note: There is no geometric cone volume formula in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Method of exhaustion to approximate π

anchor.snippet: the method of exhaustion to approximate Pi

annotations.note: Mathlib proves numerical bounds on π (e.g. `pi_gt_d6`/`pi_lt_d6`) via analytic methods rather than Archimedes' polygon-based method of exhaustion.

annotations.label: Surface area and volume of a sphere

anchor.snippet: the surface area and volume of a sphere

annotations.note: The volume of an n-ball is computed (`volume_ball`, with explicit even/odd-dimensional formulas), but the 4πr² surface-area formula of a sphere is not established.

annotations.label: Theory of conic sections

anchor.snippet: the theory of conic sections that was first outlined by Menaechmus

annotations.note: Mathlib has no theory of conic sections (ellipse/parabola/hyperbola as conics).

annotations.label: Numbers vs. magnitudes

anchor.snippet: separated numbers

annotations.note: The conceptual Greek distinction between discrete number and continuous magnitude is not a formalizable Mathlib statement.

annotations.label: Euclidean algorithm for gcd

anchor.snippet: for computing the greatest common divisor of two numbers

annotations.note: `Nat.gcd` is defined by the Euclidean recursion with the characteristic recurrence `Nat.gcd_rec`.

annotations.label: Infinitude of primes

anchor.snippet: a proof implying the infinitude of primes

annotations.note: Euclid's theorem on the infinitude of primes is `Nat.exists_infinite_primes` (also `Nat.infinite_setOf_prime`).

annotations.label: Odd times even is even

anchor.snippet: odd times even is even

annotations.note: `Even.mul_right`/`Even.mul_left` show an even number times anything is even, which subsumes odd×even.

annotations.label: Odd dividing an even number divides its half

anchor.snippet: if an odd number measures

annotations.note: The exact Euclid IX.30 statement is absent, but it follows immediately from coprimality cancellation (`Coprime.dvd_of_dvd_mul_left`) since an odd number is coprime to 2.

annotations.label: Polygonal (figurate) numbers

anchor.snippet: polygonal or figurate numbers

annotations.note: Mathlib has no definition of polygonal/figurate (triangular, square, etc.) numbers.

annotations.label: Congruences

anchor.snippet: the idea of congruences

annotations.note: Modular congruence is formalized as `Int.ModEq`/`Nat.ModEq` (notation `a ≡ b [ZMOD n]`/`[MOD n]`).

annotations.label: Archimedes' cattle problem

anchor.snippet: Archimedes's cattle problem

annotations.note: The Pell equation to which the cattle problem reduces is formalized, but the cattle problem itself is not stated.

annotations.label: Three impossible construction problems

anchor.snippet: all of which are now known to be impossible with a straight edge and compass

annotations.note: Mathlib lacks a theory of straightedge-and-compass constructible numbers, so the impossibility of these three problems is not formalized.

annotations.label: Archimedean solids

anchor.snippet: semiregular polyhedra, also known as Archimedean solids

annotations.note: No definition of semiregular/Archimedean solids exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Determinate and indeterminate equations

anchor.snippet: determinate equations (those with a unique solution) and indeterminate equations (which admit of more than one solution)

annotations.note: Mathlib has no classification of equations as determinate vs. indeterminate (Diophantine).

annotations.label: Arbelos

anchor.snippet: Archimedes' construction of the arbelos

annotations.note: The arbelos figure is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Isoperimetric figures

anchor.snippet: isoperimetric figures

annotations.note: No isoperimetric definitions or inequality are present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Descartes' theorem

anchor.snippet: proved what is now called Descartes' theorem

annotations.note: Mathlib has Descartes' Rule of Signs but not Descartes' (circle/angular-defect) theorem referenced here.

annotations.label: Fermat's Last Theorem

anchor.snippet: Fermat's Last Theorem

annotations.note: The statement `FermatLastTheorem` is defined and special cases (`fermatLastTheoremThree`, n=4) are proved, but the general theorem is not proved in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Dedekind cut

anchor.snippet: the Dedekind cut , a method of constructing the real numbers

annotations.note: `DedekindCut` is defined as the Concept lattice of `≤` for any preorder, with `DedekindCut ℚ ≃o EReal`.
